compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Fix pushed as Gerrit change 448 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/448). Rewrites the langfuse-trace job to validate current write-only OTLP tracing: installs [dev,tracing], replaces the SDK readiness probe with a REST curl (stderr no longer suppressed), and rewrites test_llm_trace.py to assert a NEW ingested trace with observations (id set-diff) instead of a removed trace_id round-trip. Verified end-to-end locally against docker-compose.langfuse.yml with a real review: GREEN on export, RED under a setup_tracing no-op mutation. Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The `langfuse tracing round-trip (ephemeral self-hosted)` job of the `external-integration`
workflow has been broken since the d6d1 cutover (2026-06-22) and tests behavior that no
longer exists. Two independent breakages:

1. **Readiness probe imports an uninstalled SDK.** `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml:128`
   runs `from langfuse import get_client; assert get_client().auth_check()`, but the `langfuse`
   Python SDK was dropped from `[agents]` in c282b2950. `pip install -e '.[dev]'` no longer
   installs it, so all 24 readiness iterations raise `ModuleNotFoundError` — hidden by
   `2>/dev/null`, masquerading as "Langfuse project did not initialize in time".

2. **Test asserts a removed trace_id round-trip.** `tests/external/test_llm_trace.py:100` asserts
   `result["trace_id"]` is truthy and fetches `GET /api/public/traces/{id}`. The runner now
   hardcodes `trace_id=None` (src/rebar/llm/runner.py:391); the SDK trace-id capture was deleted
   in 2e9bd4205 when tracing became write-only OTLP export (src/rebar/llm/tracing.py). Also the
   job never installs `[tracing]`, so `setup_tracing` no-ops and no spans are exported at all.

## Decision (user, 2026-07-09)

Rewrite the job to validate CURRENT behavior (write-only OTLP span export to Langfuse), not the
removed SDK trace_id round-trip.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Trigger the `external-integration` workflow (langfuse-trace job).
2. Observe "Wait for Langfuse to be ready" time out after 120s ("project did not initialize in time").

Expected: readiness passes; a review run's OTLP spans land in Langfuse and the test confirms it.
Actual: readiness fails on a hidden ModuleNotFoundError; the test would fail on the None trace_id anyway.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Readiness probe no longer imports the langfuse SDK; uses a REST check (e.g. `curl -fsS -u pk:sk .../api/public/projects`) and does not mask stderr.
- [ ] The langfuse-trace job installs the `[tracing]` extra so OTLP export actually fires.
- [ ] `tests/external/test_llm_trace.py` asserts OTLP spans for a review run are ingested by Langfuse (fetched via REST), with no reliance on a runner-surfaced `trace_id`.
- [ ] Verified end-to-end: the rewritten test passes against the docker-compose.langfuse.yml stack with a real review call, and the external-integration GitHub Action's langfuse-trace job runs successfully.
compiled_state.title: langfuse-trace job tests removed behavior + readiness probe imports uninstalled SDK
